# MangaViewer Copilot Instructions

## Project Overview

Web-based manga reading manager. React frontend + Express backend + SQLite, supporting folders and archives (ZIP/CBZ/RAR/CBR/7Z).

## Commands

```bash
# Root — start both frontend (port 3000) and backend (port 5002) concurrently
npm run dev
npm start          # same as dev

# Build frontend for production (backend then serves it statically)
npm run build

# Backend only
cd backend && npm run dev   # nodemon (auto-reload)
cd backend && npm start     # plain node

# Frontend only
cd frontend && npm start    # CRA dev server
cd frontend && npm run build
```

No test suite exists. (Backend has `searchParser.test.js` unit tests via Jest.)

## Architecture

```
Root npm scripts (concurrently)
├── backend/          Express + better-sqlite3
│   ├── src/index.js          Entry: mounts API, OPDS, static, SPA fallback
│   ├── src/db/database.js    SQLite schema + init + legacy migration
│   ├── src/services/
│   │   ├── archiveService.js Archive reading (ZIP/RAR/7Z) + cover generation via sharp
│   │   ├── scanService.js    Root dir scan → upserts archives table
│   │   └── scanTimer.js      Auto-scan timer (extracted from index.js to avoid circular deps)
│   └── src/routes/           One file per resource, all mounted at /api via api.js
│       └── opdsRoutes.js     Mounted at / (not /api) for OPDS protocol compatibility
└── frontend/         React 19 + React Router v7 (CRA)
    ├── src/utils/api.js      Single API client — all components call this, never fetch directly
    ├── src/hooks/            Custom hooks (useReaderKeyboard.js)
    ├── src/components/       Shared components (LazyImage, Toast, ErrorBoundary)
    ├── src/App.js            Router, theme state, sidebar layout
    ├── src/pages/            Library, Reader, History, Settings
    └── src/index.css         All CSS vars / theme switching via data-theme attribute on <html>
```

**Request flow (dev):** Frontend dev server proxies `/api/*` → `localhost:5002` (set via `"proxy"` in `frontend/package.json`). In production, Express serves `frontend/build/` as static files and handles the SPA catch-all.

**Two archive types behave differently:**
- `folder` — images served directly from filesystem; page list built by reading the directory at request time
- `zip/cbz/rar/cbr/7z` — page list stored in `pages` table; image bytes extracted on-demand by `archiveService`

**Runtime data** (`backend/data/`) is generated at startup and not committed:
- `manhuaviewer.db` — SQLite database
- Thumbnail cache — covers generated by `sharp` and cached here

## Key Conventions

**Database access is synchronous.** `better-sqlite3` is sync-only; all DB calls in route handlers use `.prepare().get()` / `.all()` / `.run()` — never `await`.

**Settings are key-value rows** in the `settings` table. Defaults are written at startup in `database.js`. To add a new setting: add a default there and read/write via `GET|PUT /api/settings`.

**Search syntax is server-side.** `archiveRoutes.js` contains `parseSearchSyntax()` which parses a custom DSL:
- `keyword` — fuzzy title match
- `tag:name` / `artist:name` — namespace:value tag filter
- `-keyword` / `-tag:name` — exclusion

**Theme is client-side only.** Stored in `localStorage`, applied as `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`. CSS variables for all three themes (`light`, `dark`, `eye-care`) are defined in `index.css`.

**Auto-scan timer** lives in `services/scanTimer.js` (started from `index.js` on boot) and restarts whenever settings change via `PUT /api/settings` (the settings route calls `startAutoScanTimer()` on update).

**OPDS routes** are mounted at `/` (not `/api`), so they must not conflict with Express static file serving — the static middleware runs before OPDS in `index.js`.

**Adding a new API route:**
1. Create `backend/src/routes/newRoutes.js`
2. `require` and `router.use('/', newRoutes)` in `routes/api.js`
3. Add corresponding methods to `frontend/src/utils/api.js`
